assertions.description: SDIO 4-bit bus detected with all 6 signals (CLK, CMD, D0-D3) on correctly named nets. I2C SCL/SDA found on CM4 module pins I2C_SCL0/I2C_SDA0 with no external pull-ups (accurate — template board). TPD2EUSB30 correctly identified as esd_ic protecting USB_D+/D-. PMOS Q1 (DMP2066LSN) detected with correct gate_net=SD_PWR_ON, drain_net=+3V3, source_net=SD_PWR. Schematic-to-PCB count matches at 45.

INCORRECT: The design_observations USB_D- entry has has_esd_protection=false, but U6 (TPD2EUSB30) is present on the USB_D- net (pin 2). The bug is in signal_detectors.py line 3499: 'protected_nets = {p["protected_net"] for p in results.get("protection_devices", [])}' uses the singular protected_net field (which is only 'USB_D+'), not the plural protected_nets list (['USB_D+', 'USB_D-', '__unnamed_90']). The fix is to collect all protected_nets entries. Affects any multi-rail ESD protection device.

INCORRECT: The transistor_circuit entry for Q1 (DMP2066LSN) includes a led_driver sub-object referencing D3 ('Activity LED') with power_rail='+3V3'. However D3 is on net PI_LED_nACT (driven directly by the CM4 module, not by Q1). The actual load on Q1's source net (SD_PWR) includes D4 ('SD ON' LED) and J2 (SD card connector). The load_type='led' is triggered by D4 on SD_PWR, but the led_driver association logic looks more broadly and picks up the unrelated D3. The primary load is the SD card (J2 connector), not an LED.

INCORRECT: The Raspberry Pi CM4 module U5 (Compute_Module_4_Functional) is split into 6 KiCad units. Each unit generates a separate subcircuit entry, resulting in 6 identical U5 entries in the subcircuits list. The bus_analysis correctly deduplicates via the KH-148 fix, but the subcircuits generation lacks the same deduplication. The correct output should have 1 U5 subcircuit entry (with the combined neighbor context). This affects any multi-unit part in a schematic.
